column1: # Baseline TCP Priority Group Completion Analysis
# This experiment shows standard TCP behavior without CATS queue jumping.
# Priority groups are sent in REVERSE order but TCP delivers in FIFO order.
# Priority tags are preserved but ignored by standard TCP.
# SENDING ORDER (same as CATS experiment):
# 1st: P4 Analytics/Tracking (150KB) - LARGEST, lowest priority
# 2nd: P3 Images/Media (60KB) - large
# 3rd: P2 Application JS (40KB) - medium
# 4th: P1 CSS Framework (25KB) - small
# 5th: P0 Critical HTML/CSS (8KB) - SMALLEST, highest priority
# EXPECTED TCP DELIVERY ORDER (FIFO):
# P4 completes first despite being lowest priority
# P3, P2, P1 complete in send order
# P0 completes last despite being highest priority
# This demonstrates the baseline TCP behavior:
# No priority awareness - delivery follows send order
# Critical content (P0) waits behind bulk data (P4)
# TSV FILE FORMAT AND INTERPRETATION GUIDE:
# Columns:
# 1. SimTime(s)        - Absolute simulation time when group completed
# 2. PageID           - Page identifier (always 0 in single-page experiment)
# 3. Priority         - CATS priority level (0=highest, 4=lowest)
# 4. CompletionTime(ms) - Relative completion time from first byte received
# 5. Event            - Always 'GROUP_COMPLETED' for this experiment
# HOW TO INTERPRET THE RESULTS:
# - Lower CompletionTime(ms) = Faster delivery
# - TCP FIFO: P4 should have lowest CompletionTime (fastest)
# - TCP FIFO: P0 should have highest CompletionTime (slowest)
# - TCP Success: P4 < P3 < P2 < P1 < P0 completion times
# - Compare with CATS: CATS should show P0 < P1 < P2 < P3 < P4
# Format: SimTime(s)
